motivation: a105829b honest C3: order saturation alone cannot close text-heavy gap (v2 48% > v3-o2 45% > v3-o3 41%). NEW dimension = LZ window enlarge 32KB→64KB (window-size axis, NOT order axis). 78.30% projected pre-v4, gap 1.70pp residual; v4 hypothesis = capture long-range repetition >32KB.

F-A18-V4-1.overall: NOT TRIPPED across 3 production text-heavy ≥16KB corpora; v4 ≥ v2 to within ±0.2pp

finding: v4-window64 algorithmic novelty CONFIRMED in principle (synthetic long_range_80k: +22.9pp v4 vs v2) but FALSIFIED as 80%-target close axis in production: dispatcher min-of-6 picks v1 or v2 on every real text-heavy corpus measured (4K/8K/16K/26K/79K). The text-heavy class CEILING on production 79K corpora is a SHORT-RANGE-MATCH problem, not a long-range-repetition problem — a 64KB window cannot help if the entropic structure terminates within 32KB.
aggregate_impact: +0.46pp on n6_atlas_79K from v4 over v2 isolated; 0pp post-dispatcher (v1 still wins). 80% target NOT achieved by this ω-cycle.
next_axis_candidates: A18 v5 = 128KB window (3-byte distance, wire-format break, ~+150 LoC) — only viable if a corpus class with cross-64KB repetition is found in 6-repo aggregate
A18 v6 = LZ greedy → optimal parsing (Storer-Szymanski lazy match, +200 LoC) — orthogonal to window axis, attacks within-32KB sub-optimality
Different module: A26 = order-4 PPM-D escape (full sparse PPM, ~500 LoC) — addresses entropy-coder ceiling not LZ ceiling

verdict: v4 LIVE FIRE measured + falsifiers preregistered + 80% target NOT achieved (gap 1.70pp residual UNCHANGED)
